questions.question: Explain the primary purpose of the 'this' keyword in Java and provide one simple code example demonstrating its use to distinguish between an instance variable and a method parameter.
keyPoints: Used to refer to the current class instance
Resolves naming conflicts between instance variables and parameters
Example: 'this.variableName = variableName;' in a constructor or setter method

questions.question: Describe three distinct uses of the 'this' keyword in Java, providing a brief code snippet or explanation for each use case.
keyPoints: To refer to current instance variables (to avoid shadowing)
To invoke current class methods (can be implicit, but explicit for clarity)
To invoke current class constructors (using 'this()' for constructor chaining)

questions.question: Analyze a scenario where the 'this' keyword is passed as an argument to a method. Explain the purpose, potential use cases, and any important considerations regarding object state when doing this.
keyPoints: Allows an object to pass a reference to itself to an external method
Common in event handling or callback mechanisms
The external method can then call back to the object's methods
Consideration: Must ensure the object is in a valid state when passed
Risk: Can lead to tightly coupled code if not designed carefully
